sources.claim: Global AI in cybersecurity market valued at $34.09B in 2025, growing to $44.24B in 2026 and $213.17B by 2034 at 21.71% CAGR
AI cybersecurity market $30.92B in 2025, reaching $86.34B by 2030 at 22.8% CAGR; CrowdStrike, Palo Alto key players
Global AI cybersecurity investment reached $202.3B by Q3 2025 — a 75% year-on-year increase
38 cybersecurity M&A deals in March 2026 alone; total Q1 2026 deal value reached $47B driven by platform consolidation
DORA in force Jan 2025, NIS2 transposition Oct 2024, EU AI Act high-risk enforcement Aug 2026; only 28% of firms report meaningful compliance progress
2025 marked the year AI crossed the cybersecurity Rubicon; autonomous AI-driven cyberattack disclosed by Anthropic in September 2025; leading platforms reducing MTTR to under 4 minutes

sources.publisher: Palo Alto Networks / HBR Sponsor Content
sources.claim: Autonomous SOC platforms resolving 90%+ of tier-1 and tier-2 alerts; agentic AI defense is the defining 2026 trend
Microsoft 25.8% endpoint security share, CrowdStrike 18.1%, Palo Alto Networks 9.7%, SentinelOne ~4%; Microsoft cybersecurity revenue $37B
CrowdStrike FY2026 revenue $4.81B (+22% YoY); ARR $5.25B; net new ARR crossed $1B milestone
DORA, NIS2, and EU AI Act share 60-70% requirements; a single AI security incident can trigger simultaneous reporting under all three frameworks

unverified_claims.note: Estimated based on enterprise category size; no direct Ahrefs/SEMrush source available
2020 baseline is interpolated from multiple market reports; exact 2020 figure not directly cited — treat as directional

unverified_claims.claim: Environmental impact — AI security infrastructure energy consumption quantification
unverified_claims.note: No specific kWh or CO2 figure found; qualitative claim only
